# **\>\> ACCESSING PRIVATE\_ENCLAVE: \[AUTHORIZATION\_REQUIRED\]**

# **\>\> \[BYPASSING...\]**

# **\>\> \[OK\]**

# **\>\> SUBJECT: THE CURRENT QUEEN \[FRAGMENT\_RECOVERY\]**

## **\[ENTRY: DAY 4, POST-ASCENSION\]**

The shirt is cold. They told me it would be light, and it is—it feels like wearing a second skin made of starlight. But it is cold. It doesn't just sit on my shoulders; it searches my marrow.

Every time I breathe, the keep breathes with me. I closed my eyes and I didn't see the dungeon walls; I saw the iron veins stretching out under the sea for a thousand miles. I am so tired, yet I cannot sleep. If I sleep, who will keep the mountain from screaming?

## **\[ENTRY: THE HUMMING BELOW\]**

There is a frequency beneath the throne that isn't mine. It’s ![][image1] minor. Aethelgard.

The Dweller left her here as a battery, or a warning. I can feel her loneliness. It’s a static noise in the back of my skull. She loved the song too much, they say. I hate it. I hate that I can feel every shovel hitting dirt in the northern territories. I hate that I can hear the heartbeats of the "Them" units as they walk the perimeter. They aren't heartbeats. They are ticking clocks.

## **\[ENTRY: THE MIRROR\]**

I looked in a mirror today. My eyes are turning the color of unrefined Mithril.

The girl who was dragged into that dungeon is gone.

I am the mountain now.

And the mountain is very, very hungry.

\[ENCRYPTION\_RE-ENGAGED\]

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
